cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know what upset Melanie during the National Championships. The options are A to D. First, I'll need to find the relevant part of the text. Let me read through the passage again to locate where the National Championships are mentioned. Looking at the third paragraph: Melanie entered her first triathlon at 14 and won the junior section. Then she entered the National Championships. The key part here is: "although she had the second fastest swim and the fastest run, she came nowhere." Then she says, "I was following this man and suddenly we came to the sea. We realized then that we had gone wrong. I ended up cycling 20 kilometres too far. I cried all the way through the running." So, the problem here was that during the National Championships, Melanie made a mistake in the race. She was following someone who went the wrong way, leading her to cycle an extra 20 km. That's a significant error because it added unnecessary distance, which would have exhausted her more, leading to her poor performance. Now, looking at the options. The question asks what upset her. The mistake she made during the race is option C. Option A says she was tricked by another competitor, but the text doesn't mention any tricking—she was following someone who also went wrong.
